description:
<name>Generate an error–frequency summary from an application log</name>

    <description>
        You are helping a developer who is cleaning up project artefacts in
        “/home/user/dev_project”.  
        Inside “/home/user/dev_project/logs” there is a single log file called
        “server.log”.  
        The developer would like a very small report that lists how many times
        each distinct error code appears in that file.

        A valid error line in the log looks like this (spaces are literal):

            2024-05-10 10:05:12 [ERROR] [E1001] Failed to connect to database

        • The error-code is always the 5-character token inside the second pair
          of square brackets, e.g. “E1001”.  
        • Ignore any line that does not contain the string “[ERROR]”.
        • Count *all* occurrences of every distinct error-code that remains.

        Create a new plain-text file called  
        “/home/user/dev_project/logs/error_summary.log”.

        Exact output format required (this is what the automated grader will
        diff against, so follow it precisely):

        1. One error-code per line.  
        2. Each line must be:  
               <ERROR_CODE>:<COUNT>  
           with **no spaces** before or after the colon.  
        3. Lines must be sorted in ascending alphabetical order by the
           error-code (e.g. E0001 before E0002, E0999 before E1000, etc.).  
        4. After the very last line, include exactly *one* trailing newline
           character and nothing else.

        Example of the *shape* (actual numbers will of course depend on the log
        contents):

            E0123:7
            E0456:2
            E9876:1⏎   ← single trailing newline, no blank lines before/after

        You may use whatever shell utilities you like, but the entire task can
        be completed with a short pipeline of 3–4 commands (e.g. grep, cut,
        sort, uniq, awk, etc.).

        Do **not** modify “server.log”; your script or commands should only
        read it and then create the new “error_summary.log” file in the same
        “logs” directory.
    </description>
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing file placed by the test harness
    -->
    <initialFile path="/home/user/dev_project/logs/server.log"><![CDATA[
2024-05-10 10:00:01 [INFO] Server started
2024-05-10 10:05:12 [ERROR] [E1001] Failed to connect to database
2024-05-10 10:06:05 [WARN] Low disk space
2024-05-10 10:07:45 [ERROR] [E2345] Timeout while processing request
2024-05-10 10:08:30 [ERROR] [E1001] Failed to connect to database
2024-05-10 10:09:01 [INFO] User login successful
2024-05-10 10:10:15 [ERROR] [E7777] Null pointer exception
2024-05-10 10:15:00 [INFO] Cache cleared
2024-05-10 10:20:22 [ERROR] [E2345] Timeout while processing request
2024-05-10 10:25:33 [INFO] Session terminated
]]></initialFile>

    <!--
        File the agent must create
    -->
    <expectedFile path="/home/user/dev_project/logs/error_summary.log"><![CDATA[
E1001:2
E2345:2
E7777:1
]]></expectedFile>
name: task_000000_90a53733
